title: GitHub issue not getting created from anshimitt telegram
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

GitHub issue not getting created from anshimitt telegram

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The Telegram bot for user "anshimitt" is failing to create GitHub issues when commands are sent.
- Affected component: bot.js or the GitHub issue creation pipeline
- Expected: When a user sends a bug/feature request via Telegram, a GitHub issue should be created automatically
- Current: Issues are not being created for this specific Telegram user
- Edge cases: Could be a username/chat_id mapping issue, API auth issue, or rate limiting
- Acceptance criteria: GitHub issues are successfully created when anshimitt sends requests via Telegram bot
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- bot/bot.js: Refactored isAllowed() to accept full msg object and match by both username AND numeric Telegram user ID. Added @-prefix stripping for ALLOWED_USERS parsing. Added diagnostic logging on auth failures (username, user_id, chat_id). Updated all 9 callers from isAllowed(msg.from.username) to isAllowed(msg). Updated startup logs to show both usernames and user IDs.

Root cause: isAllowed() only matched by msg.from.username, which is optional (users without a Telegram username) and mutable (users can change it). If anshimitt's actual Telegram username didn't exactly match the .env entry, auth would silently fail with no logging.

Fix: isAllowed() now checks both username and user_id. Operators can add numeric Telegram user IDs to ALLOWED_USERS for reliable matching. Auth failures now log the attempted username and user_id for easy diagnosis.

Manual step needed: Restart the bot process (kill existing, launchd will restart). Optionally add anshimitt's numeric Telegram user ID to ALLOWED_USERS in .env for bulletproof matching.

title: welcome screen should be converted as guide can be revisited from settings
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

welcome screen should be converted as guide can be revisited from settings. It can be more elaborate than welcome screen as well and can have more slides.

---

**Category:** `bug` `ui` `content`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The welcome/onboarding screen should be converted into a "Guide" that users can revisit from the Settings page, with more elaborate content and additional slides.
- Affected component: Welcome screen component and Settings page in src/
- Expected: A "Guide" option in Settings that opens a more detailed version of the welcome carousel with additional slides
- Current: Welcome screen is only shown on first launch and cannot be revisited
- Edge cases: Guide should work for both new and existing users; should not reset onboarding state; can have more slides than the initial welcome
- Acceptance criteria: Settings page has a "Guide" option that opens an elaborate, multi-slide guide based on the welcome screen content
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/features/settings/Settings.tsx: Added onShowGuide prop and Guide section with View Guide button
- src/App.tsx: Added showGuideFromSettings state, passed onShowGuide to Settings and showGuideFromSettings/onGuideShown to HomeTab
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Added showGuideFromSettings/onGuideShown props with useEffect to trigger welcome carousel

Approach: When user taps View Guide in Settings, App closes Settings, navigates to home tab, and sets showGuideFromSettings=true. HomeTab detects this via useEffect and opens the welcome carousel.

title: my wellness graphs are not there
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

my wellness graphs are not there. My stats should be available in all stats . One graph with multiple option for mother.

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Mother/parent wellness graphs are missing from the stats section. User wants wellness tracking stats available in the "All Stats" view with a graph showing multiple wellness metrics.
- Affected component: Stats/analytics section in src/ — likely an AllStats or WellnessStats component
- Expected: A wellness graph for mother/parent data should appear in the All Stats section, with options to toggle between different wellness metrics
- Current: No wellness graphs are shown for the mother/parent
- Edge cases: Handle case where no wellness data exists yet; support multiple metric types on one graph; responsive chart sizing
- Acceptance criteria: All Stats section includes a mother wellness graph with selectable metrics (e.g., mood, sleep, hydration)
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/features/wellness/MomCare.tsx: Added momcare_history persistence — saves daily wellness data to history array (last 90 days) on every change
- src/features/stats/StatsView.tsx: Added wellness chart section with mood/sleep/meals/progress metrics using BarChart and MetricPills components

Approach: MomCare now writes to both momcare_today (current day) and momcare_history (array of all days). StatsView loads the history on mount and displays a bar chart with selectable metrics. Weekly/monthly periods show averaged values.

title: I like the hero widget background
body:

I like the hero widget background. can the background have subtle animation?

**Source:** Telegram Bot

enhanced_description:
User requests a subtle background animation on the hero widget at the top of the home screen.
- Affected component: Hero widget/banner component on the home page in src/
- Expected: The hero widget background should have a gentle, subtle CSS or JS animation (e.g., gradient shift, particle effect, or slow movement)
- Current: Hero widget has a static background
- Edge cases: Animation should be performant and not drain battery; should respect reduced-motion preferences; should not distract from content
- Acceptance criteria: Hero widget background has a subtle, smooth animation that enhances visual appeal without impacting performance
implementation_notes:

- src/styles/base.css: Added heroGradientShift and heroOrbFloat keyframe animations
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Applied gradient shift animation to hero background (12s cycle), added float animation to decorative orbs with staggered timing

Approach: Pure CSS animations for zero JS overhead. Gradient extends to 200% backgroundSize and shifts position. Decorative orbs float with different durations and delays for organic feel.

title: long press reveal quick log animation for in and close is not professional

## ✨ Feature Request

long press reveal quick log animation for in and close is not professional

**Source:** Telegram Bot

enhanced_description:
The long-press animation that reveals the quick log overlay has unprofessional-looking open and close transitions.
- Affected component: Quick log long-press reveal animation in src/ (likely a QuickLog or LongPressOverlay component)
- Expected: Smooth, polished enter and exit animations for the quick log overlay (e.g., proper easing, consistent timing, no jank)
- Current: The open and close animations look rough or unprofessional

- Acceptance criteria: Quick log long-press reveal has professional, smooth open and close animations with proper easing curves
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/styles/base.css: Added qlInfoOpen (scale+blur spring animation) and qlInfoClose (scale-down fade) keyframes
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Applied qlInfoOpen animation to info panel, added animated close with qlInfoClose before removing panel

Approach: Open uses a spring-like cubic-bezier curve with scale overshoot and blur-to-sharp for a premium feel. Close animates out before unmounting via setTimeout.

title: the hero widget should provide info on last feed below feed count without a l...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

the hero widget should provide info on last feed below feed count without a lot of clutter along with the side info / formula/ pumped etc.

---

**Category:** `enhancement` `content` `feeding`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Hero widget feed stat card should show last feed details (type and time) below the feed count, without adding clutter. Feed types include Breast L/R, Formula, Pumped Milk.
- Affected component: Hero section in src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx — the feed stat card (around line 1000-1020)
- Expected: Below the feed count number, show a compact line like '🤱 L · 2:30pm' or '🍼 Formula · 1:15pm' for the most recent feed
- Current: Only shows feed count number and optional oz/ml total
- Edge cases: No feeds today (show nothing extra), timer active (already shows timer), breast vs formula vs pumped rendering
- Acceptance criteria: Last feed type and time shown compactly below count in hero, only when not in timer mode and feeds exist
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Added lastFeedToday and lastFeedLabel computed from today's feeds. Shows compact 'type · time' line (e.g. '🤱 L · 2:30 PM' or '🍼 Formula · 1:15 PM') below feed count in hero widget. Only visible when feeds exist today and timer is not active. Handles all feed types: Breast L/R, Formula, Pumped Milk, Solids.
